audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Deuteronomy / Devarim return, heart-circumcision, near-word, and choose-life discourse, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for return after blessing and curse, gathering from exile, heart circumcision, restored abundance, Torah accessibility, life/death choice, heaven-and-earth witness, and covenant clinging.

verses.restored: It shall be, when all these words come upon you, blessing and curse that I set before you, and you bring them back to your heart among all nations where YHWH your Divine has driven you,
and you return to YHWH your Divine and listen to his voice according to all I command you today, you and your children, with all your heart and all your soul/life,
then YHWH your Divine will return your captivity/restore your fortunes and have compassion on you, and he will return and gather you from all peoples where YHWH your Divine scattered you.
If your scattered ones are at end of heavens, from there YHWH your Divine will gather you, and from there he will take you.
YHWH your Divine will bring you into land your fathers possessed, and you shall possess it; he will do good to you and multiply you more than your fathers.
YHWH your Divine will circumcise your heart and heart of your seed, to love YHWH your Divine with all your heart and all your soul/life, so that you may live.
YHWH your Divine will place all these oaths/curses upon your enemies and upon those hating you who persecuted you.
You shall return and listen to voice of YHWH and do all his commands that I command you today.

YHWH your Divine will make you abound in all work of your hand, fruit of your womb, fruit of your livestock, and fruit of your ground, for good; for YHWH will return to rejoice over you for good, as he rejoiced over your fathers,

when you listen to voice of YHWH your Divine, keeping his commands and statutes written in this scroll of Torah/instruction, when you return to YHWH your Divine with all your heart and all your soul/life.
For this command that I command you today is not too difficult/wonderful for you, and it is not far away.
It is not in heavens, that you should say, Who will go up for us to heavens and take it for us and make us hear it, so we may do it?
And it is not beyond sea, that you should say, Who will cross for us beyond sea and take it for us and make us hear it, so we may do it?
For word is very near to you, in your mouth and in your heart, to do it.
See, I have set before you today life and good, death and evil.

For I command you today to love YHWH your Divine, to walk in his ways, and to keep his commands, statutes, and judgments, so you may live and multiply and YHWH your Divine may bless you in land where you are entering to possess it.
But if your heart turns away and you do not listen, and you are drawn away and bow to other gods and serve them,
I declare to you today that you shall surely perish; you shall not lengthen days upon ground where you are crossing Jordan to enter and possess it.
I call heavens and earth as witnesses against you today: life and death I have set before you, blessing and curse. Choose life, so you and your seed may live,

by loving YHWH your Divine, listening to his voice, and clinging to him, for he is your life and length of your days, to dwell upon ground YHWH swore to your fathers, to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, to give them.

translation_notes: Return/teshuvah follows even the full catastrophe of chapter 28; exile is not permanent rejection, and covenant continuity remains intact.
The phrase commonly rendered 'restore your fortunes' can also be heard as YHWH returning with the captivity, a major Jewish interpretive insight emphasizing Divine presence with the exiled.
Heart circumcision is an internal Jewish covenant metaphor and must never be used to supersede embodied Jewish circumcision or portray Judaism as merely external.
Verse 7's transfer of curses to persecutors is restoration rhetoric, not permission for private revenge or collective punishment.
The command is near—in mouth and heart—and does not require an inaccessible heavenly or overseas mediator.
'Choose life' is a communal covenant appeal, not a tool for blaming suicidal, depressed, ill, disabled, traumatized, or endangered people for circumstances beyond their control.
Life and death language must not be reduced to prosperity outcomes; it names a covenant orientation toward sustained communal life.
safety_note: Do not use Deuteronomy 30 for supersessionism, revenge, suicide blame, disability blame, prosperity theology, or coercive demands that traumatized people simply choose differently. Center return, compassion, gathering, accessibility of Torah, renewed heart, and the communal obligation to choose conditions in which people and descendants can live.
